titlestmt.titleproper: Joe Austin graffiti and street art collection8159

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2024-09-20 00:26:24 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: Joe Austin graffiti and street art collection

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The collection features books, zines, videos, and recorded interviews covering the graffiti and street art movement, including nearly 600 zine issues across 345 zine titles, 156 books, 41 early graffiti VHS/DVD "magazines" (6 DVD and 35 VHS tapes) from the early 90s, and approximately 52 audio tapes preserving interviews with ca 40 writers collected between 1991 and 1996 (44 cassette tapes and 8 micro cassettes). Writers interviewed include Futura, Iz the Wiz, Phase 2, Vulcan, SEEN, and Zephyr. Also interviewed are those knowledgeable about or involved in aspects of graffiti activity in NYC, including Pat DiLillo, founder of the Phun Factory and the 5Pointz project in Queens, author and photographer Jack Stewart, journalist and documentarian Sacha Jenkins, former New York City mayor Ed Koch, and David Gunn, former president of the New York City Transit Authority (1984 to 1990).

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Joe Austin is a scholar of graffiti and street art and the author of Taking the Train: How Graffiti Art Became an Urban Crisis in New York City, 1970-1990 (Columbia, 2001). He retired in 2022 as Associate Professor, Emeritus, from the University of Wisconsin, Milwaukee's Department of History.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Joe Austin graffiti and street art collection, #8159. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Due to the fragility and potential degradation of moving image and sound recordings, viewing and listening is limited to items that have been digitized. If an item is in another media format, you may request to have the item digitized for access. Information on ordering access copies may be found on the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections webpage. Use of digitized audio visual materials is limited to on-site access only.

controlaccess.subject: Graffiti artists.
Street art -- New York (State) -- New York.
